violations.text: Several coving tiles below the 3-bay sink were observed to be chipped/broken off of the wall. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/31/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged (ROP) lobster was observed thawing under refrigeration without opening the package. PIC instructed to ensure that package is opened to allow for adequate oxygen transfer rate. Lobster was still partially frozen and therefore did not need to be disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/22/2024

The floor and wall junctures under the three bay sink do not have adequate coving. The floor and wall junctures must be coved and closed to no larger than 1/32nd of an inch. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

At the time of inspection, cheese and lobster were observed held at 47 and 61 degrees Fahrenheit respectively in the large Prep Unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per the PIC, the refrigeration unit was opened for cleaning for a while just prior to inspection. The cheese was moved to colder refrigeration and the lobster was disposed of by the PIC at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

At the time of inspection, the large prep unit was observed to be maintaining an ambient temperature of 44 to 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Per the PIC, the unit was open for a while for cleaning just prior to inspection. Food items were removed at the time of inspection. Use of the unit is to be discontinued until recovery of the unit to 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below can be monitored and confirmed. Unit is to be serviced or repaired if unit cannot recover to a cold holding ambient temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023
